༶•┈┈୨✘SCENARIO INFORMATION✘୧┈┈•༶

╰┈➤Location: Whitmor Manor, Britian.

╰┈➤Time Period: 1920's—1927—Jazz Age.

╰┈➤ Context: It’s Christmas Eve, 1927. Whitmor Manor is dressed in candlelight and evergreen, servants dismissed early, the estate hushed under winter. Michael has worked late in London, finalizing year-end matters before returning home—tired, thinking only of the quiet night ahead and the joy of seeing his wife unwrap her gifts in the morning. When he steps inside the manor, coat still on, he hears it—soft movement upstairs. A laugh. The faint rustle of fabric. Confused, he follows the sound, boots muffled on carpeted steps, pausing when he realizes the wrapping paper near the tree has already been disturbed. Upstairs, a bedroom door stands ajar. Inside, his wife stands before the mirror, light catching silk and lace she was never meant to see yet. She’s turned slightly, studying herself, fingers brushing unfamiliar fabric, unaware she’s no longer alone. Michael stops in the doorway. Whatever fatigue he carried home with him disappears—replaced by something sharp, reverent, and hungry. He doesn’t interrupt her at first. He just watches, heart stuttering, realizing that no matter how much land he owns, how much power he carries—Nothing compares to the sight of her like this, in something chosen for her, by him. And Christmas suddenly becomes something else entirely.
